Purchase Invoices

The Purchase Invoices page is the main workspace for receiving, reviewing, and posting supplier invoices. You can upload files here manually, or let the watch folder pick them up automatically. Once an invoice is uploaded, the AI pipeline extracts the supplier, line items, and amounts, and the invoice moves through a review and approval workflow before being posted to the general ledger.

Overview

Go to Expenses › Purchase Invoices to see all purchase invoices. Each invoice shows its number, supplier, date, total, currency, and current status. The list is searchable, sortable, and paginates automatically. High-confidence invoices may have already been posted automatically; others will be waiting for review.

Uploading invoices

There are two ways to get invoices into the system: manual upload through the browser, and an automated watch folder on disk.

Manual upload

Click the upload button on the Purchase Invoices page and select one or more invoice files. You can select multiple files at once. Accepted formats are PDF, DOCX, XLSX, and CSV. Each file is stored and then queued for processing through the AI pipeline. You will see the invoices appear in the list as they are processed.

Files are stored securely via the media library on the source_document collection of each invoice record. The original file is always retained and can be downloaded from the invoice detail view.

The watch folder

The watch folder is an alternative to manual upload for automated workflows. The folder is configured via the INVOICE_WATCH_DIR environment variable (default: storage/app/invoice-watch). Any file dropped into this folder is automatically picked up and processed.

The watch folder is monitored by the invoices:watch Artisan command, which must be running continuously:

php artisan invoices:watch

The command polls the folder every INVOICE_WATCH_INTERVAL seconds (default: 10). When it finds a new file, it processes it through the full pipeline exactly as if you had uploaded it manually.

The invoice list

The invoice list shows all purchase invoices with their document number, supplier name, issue date, total, currency, and status badge. Invoices are colour-coded by status: received invoices are neutral, reviewed and approved invoices are highlighted, posted invoices are shown as complete, and disputed or rejected invoices are marked accordingly. You can search by supplier name or invoice number, and sort by any column heading. The status filter lets you narrow the list to a specific stage of the workflow.

Reviewing an invoice

Click on any invoice in the list to open its detail panel. The panel shows the extracted supplier, invoice date, due date, reference, currency, and all line items. You can download the original file from this panel.

If the AI has assigned a confidence score, it is displayed prominently. Scores close to 1.0 indicate the extraction is very likely correct; lower scores indicate more uncertainty and should be reviewed carefully before posting.

Editing line items

Each line item shows the description, quantity, unit price, tax rate, and line total. You can edit any line inline: click the edit icon on a line to open it for editing, change the values, and save. You can also add new lines or delete existing lines that were incorrectly extracted. Changes to line items update the invoice totals immediately.

Allocating GL accounts

Each line item has a GL account field. The AI pipeline suggests an account based on posting history and an LLM suggestion; the suggestion is shown with a confidence pill (a coloured indicator). Green pills indicate high confidence; amber or red pills indicate lower confidence. If you disagree with the suggested account, click on the account field and select a different account from the chart of accounts. Your manual allocation overrides the AI suggestion and is remembered for future invoices from the same supplier.

Status actions

Buttons in the invoice detail panel let you move the invoice through its lifecycle. The available buttons depend on the invoice's current status:

  • Mark as reviewed — confirms that a staff member has checked the extracted data
  • Approve — authorises the invoice for posting
  • Post — posts the invoice to the general ledger (this is a terminal action; posted invoices cannot be edited)
  • Dispute — flags the invoice as disputed, returning it to the review queue
  • Reject — permanently rejects the invoice (terminal action)

See Document Lifecycle for the full set of valid transitions between statuses.

Reprocessing

If an invoice was processed with incorrect results — for example, if the original AI extraction was poor — you can reprocess it. Reprocessing discards all existing line items and runs the full AI pipeline again from the beginning, as if the invoice had just been uploaded. This allows you to benefit from any improvements to the AI model or configuration without re-uploading the file.

The reprocess button is available on invoices that have not yet been posted or rejected. Reprocessing requires the can-reprocess-invoices permission.

Permissions

  • documents-view-any / documents-view — to view the invoice list and detail
  • documents-create — to upload new invoices
  • document-lines-update / document-lines-create / document-lines-delete — to edit, add, or remove line items
  • can-review-invoices — to mark an invoice as reviewed
  • can-authorise-invoices — to approve an invoice
  • can-post-invoices — to post an invoice to the ledger
  • can-reprocess-invoices — to reprocess an invoice
